'''''UFC 117: Silva vs. Sonnen''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] on August 7, 2010, at the [[Oracle Arena]] in [[Oakland, California]], United States.<ref name="ufc117">{{cite web |url=http://www.ufc.com/event/UFC117#/fight|title=UFC 117: Silva vs. Sonnen|publisher=ufc.com |date=August 7, 2010}}</ref>

==Background==
UFC President [[Dana White]] announced via [[ESPN]] on [[Jim Rome is Burning]] that Middleweight Champion [[Anderson Silva]] would face [[Chael Sonnen]] on August 7.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/18719/anderson-silva-vs-chael-sonnen-targeted-for-august-in-utah-potentially-ufc-117.mma|title=Anderson Silva vs. Chael Sonnen targeted for August in Utah, potentially UFC 117|publisher=mmajunkie.com |date=April 14, 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.sherdog.com/news/news/White-UFC-Planning-Silva-Sonnen-Showdown-23878|title=UFC Planning Silva-Sonnen Showdown|publisher=sherdog.com |date=April 14, 2010}}</ref>

White threatened to cut Silva from the UFC if he fought the same way he did in his previous bout in Abu Dhabi at [[UFC 112]]. "I'm telling you right now, if he acts like that again in the ring, I will cut him," said White. "I don't care if he's the pound for pound best fighter in the world. I don't care if he's the middleweight champion."

A bout between [[Joey Beltran]] and [[Matt Mitrione]] was reported to be in the works.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://truefighting.webs.com/apps/blog/show/3707205-beltran-vs-mitrione-in-the-works-for-ufc-117-in-oakland |title=TrueFighting.com |publisher=Truefighting.webs.com |date=May 10, 2010 |access-date=August 1, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100514221838/http://truefighting.webs.com/apps/blog/show/3707205-beltran-vs-mitrione-in-the-works-for-ufc-117-in-oakland |archive-date=May 14, 2010 }}</ref> The fight was later moved to [[UFC 119]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Marrocco |first=Steven |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19340/ufc-119-targeted-for-sept-25-in-indianapolis-mitrione-vs-beltran-on-tap.mma |title=UFC 119 targeted for Sept. 25 in Indianapolis, Mitrione vs. Beltran on tap |publisher=MMAjunkie.com |date=May 28, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120714063253/http://mmajunkie.com/news/19340/ufc-119-targeted-for-sept-25-in-indianapolis-mitrione-vs-beltran-on-tap.mma |archive-date=July 14, 2012 }}</ref>

[[Stanislav Nedkov]] was scheduled to make his UFC debut against [[Rodney Wallace (fighter)|Rodney Wallace]], but was replaced by [[Phil Davis (fighter)|Phil Davis]] after sustaining an injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/20018/phil-davis-replaces-injured-stanislav-nedkov-meets-rodney-wallace-at-ufc-117.mma|title=Phil Davis replaces injured Stanislav Nedkov, meets Rodney Wallace at UFC 117|publisher=mmajunkie.com|date=July 22, 2010|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120711144529/mmajunkie.com/news/20018/phil-davis-replaces-injured-stanislav-nedkov-meets-rodney-wallace-at-ufc-117.mma|archive-date=July 11, 2012}}</ref>

[[Thiago Silva (fighter)|Thiago Silva]] was scheduled to face [[Tim Boetsch]], but was forced off the card with an aggravated back injury.<ref name="SilvaOut">{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/20065/thiago-silva-injured-again-replacement-search-underway-for-ufc-117-slot.mma |title=Thiago Silva injured again, replacement search underway for UFC 117 slot |work=[[MMAjunkie.com]] |date=July 27, 2010 |last1=Morgan |first1=John |last2=Marrocco |first2=Steven |access-date=July 1, 2011 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110303232345/http://mmajunkie.com/news/20065/thiago-silva-injured-again-replacement-search-underway-for-ufc-117-slot.mma |archive-date=March 3, 2011 }}</ref> Silva's replacement was Todd Brown.<ref>{{cite web |last=Stupp |first=Dann |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/20068/ufc-newcomer-todd-brown-replaces-thiago-silva-at-ufc-117-meets-tim-boetsch.mma |title=UFC newcomer Todd Brown replaces Thiago Silva at UFC 117, meets Tim Boetsch |publisher=MMAjunkie.com |date=July 27, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100730013551/http://mmajunkie.com/news/20068/ufc-newcomer-todd-brown-replaces-thiago-silva-at-ufc-117-meets-tim-boetsch.mma |archive-date=July 30, 2010 }}</ref>

Dana White confirmed that the winner of the [[Jon_Fitch|Fitch]] vs. [[Thiago_Alves_(fighter)|Alves]] fight would receive a title shot.<ref>{{cite web|last=Morgan |first=John |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19807/lesnar-vs-velasquez-up-next-for-ufc-title-dos-santos-vs-nelson-winner-on-deck.mma |title=Lesnar vs. Velasquez up next for UFC title; Dos Santos vs. Nelson winner on deck |publisher=MMAjunkie.com |date=July 4, 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/20179/dana-white-confirms-ufc-117s-jon-fitch-vs-thiago-alves-winner-gets-title-shot.mma|title=Dana White confirms UFC 117s Jon Fitch vs. Thiago Alves winner gets title shot|publisher=mmajunkie.com|date=August 5, 2010|access-date=August 5, 2010|archive-date=May 25, 2012|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120525132652/http://mmajunkie.com/news/20179/dana-white-confirms-ufc-117s-jon-fitch-vs-thiago-alves-winner-gets-title-shot.mma|url-status=dead}}</ref>

The Fitch/Alves fight was changed to catchweight status after Alves weighed in at 171.5 lb; 0.5 lb over the 171 lb welterweight limit for non title fights. Alves was fined 20% of his purse.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/20168/ufc-117-live-weigh-in-results.mma|title=UFC 117 live weigh-in results|publisher=mmajunkie.com|date=August 6, 2010|access-date=August 7, 2010|archive-date=May 25, 2012|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120525132655/http://mmajunkie.com/news/20168/ufc-117-live-weigh-in-results.mma|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $60,000 bonuses.<ref name="Thomas">{{cite web |url=http://www.bloodyelbow.com/2010/8/7/1610804/ufc-117-silva-vs-sonnen-post-fight |title=UFC 117: Silva vs. Sonnen Post-Fight Press Conference |work=BloodyElbow.com |date=August 8, 2010 |last=Thomas |first=Nick |access-date=July 1, 2011 |archive-date=December 25, 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101225231332/http://www.bloodyelbow.com/2010/8/7/1610804/ufc-117-silva-vs-sonnen-post-fight |url-status=dead }}</ref>

*'''Fight of the Night: Anderson Silva vs. Chael Sonnen'''
*'''Knockout of the Night: Stefan Struve'''
*'''Submission of the Night: Anderson Silva and Matt Hughes'''

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the [[California State Athletic Commission]]. It does not include sponsor money or "locker room" bonuses often given by the UFC and also do not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/20298/ufc-117-salaries-anderson-silva-and-matt-hughes-lead-1-million-fighter-payroll.mma|title=UFC 117 salaries: Anderson Silva and Matt Hughes top $1 million fighter payroll|publisher=mmajunkie.com|date=August 16, 2010|access-date=August 16, 2010|archive-date=August 19, 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100819192600/http://mmajunkie.com/news/20298/ufc-117-salaries-anderson-silva-and-matt-hughes-lead-1-million-fighter-payroll.mma|url-status=dead}}</ref>

*'''Anderson Silva''' $200,000 (no win bonus) def. Chael Sonnen $35,000
*'''Jon Fitch''' $108,000 ($54,000 win bonus) def. Thiago Alves $60,000
*'''Clay Guida''' $56,000 ($28,000 win bonus) def. Rafael dos Anjos $12,000
*'''Matt Hughes''' $200,000 ($100,000 win bonus) def. Ricardo Almeida $35,000
*'''Junior dos Santos''' $80,000 ($40,000 win bonus) def. Roy Nelson $15,000
*'''Rick Story''' $22,000 ($11,000 win bonus) def. Dustin Hazlett $18,000
*'''Phil Davis''' $18,000 ($9,000 win bonus) def. Rodney Wallace $6,000
*'''Johny Hendricks''' $40,000 ($20,000 win bonus) def. Charlie Brenneman $8,000
*'''Tim Boetsch''' $32,000 ($16,000 win bonus) def. Todd Brown $6,000
*'''Stefan Struve''' $30,000 ($15,000 win bonus) def. Christian Morecraft $8,000
*'''Dennis Hallman''' $30,000 ($15,000 win bonus) def. Ben Saunders $12,000

^ ''Thiago Alves was reportedly fined 20 percent of his purse for failing to make the 170-pound welterweight limit. The CSAC's initial report did not include information on the penalty.''
